mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which tends to compress demand for equities and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the housing market signal suggests a strengthening demand for equities, which tends to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production signal suggests a weakening supply of equities, which tends to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the retail sales signal suggests a strengthening demand for equities, which tends to produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signal suggests an easing monetary cycle, which tends to compress discount rates applied to equity cash flows and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
